per_case.rationale: Both analyses are accurate with no fabrications: A correctly catches the Koizumi quote misattribution and the Russia-NK overstatement, but pads with two self-cancelling non-problems (June 28 date, Lianhe Zaobao). B finds more substantive, verifiable issues — the src-007 'primary deterrence responsibility' vs 'primary defense role' misattribution, the narrowed object of China's protest in src-015,

Both correctly flag the real 2-vs-3 nuclear-reactor and 40-vs-48 drowning divergences with accurate corrections; A additionally catches a genuine misquote of Castellan (article dropped the 38C/18C comparator) that B misses, and surfaces both divergences transparently. A's one flaw is mischaracterizing src-004 as listing 'Bugey, Nogent-sur-Seine, and Golfech' (src-004 attributes three reactors to N

Output B caught the single clearest article error — the unsupported 'Swiss-mediated' attribution absent from every source — corrected it cleanly, and offered four well-grounded divergences with no padding. Output A found the genuine 'released funds' framing issue and sound divergences, but padded problems_found with three self-negating non-findings plus a duplicate and missed the Swiss fabrication

Output A found two genuine, well-supported problems (the Biden-origin detail misattributed to The Atlantic, and the unsupported 'regardless of sentence' clause), applied accurate corrections, and reported real divergences with no fabrication. Output B padded problems_found with seven non-issues it concluded needed no fix, its only substantive corrections were minor duplicate citation tweaks to the

Both correctly catch the core defect (article claims a lawsuit was 'filed' while src-008 only supports a 'legal challenge'). A additionally catches a genuine, verifiable error B misses (src-008's designation is 'national security AND supply chain risk') and fixes it, but clutters problems_found with self-negating factually_incorrect flags (John Coleman, Kate Koren) that admit no error exists. Output A catches the clearest objective error (the article lists 'French' coverage though no French-language source exists; France 24 is English) and correctly fixes it, with all findings well-supported. Output B does valid subtler work on the unsupported Macron 'national fiscal sovereignty' framing and the G7 'next forum/both expected to attend' embellishment, but misses the French-language error

Both correctly flag the unsupported 'not directed at any third country' subheadline claim; A's two findings are both accurate and actionable (including a defensible timing fix), and its four divergences are well-supported. B lands one genuine extra catch (the 'nearly 10' vs 'more than 10' number discrepancy) but clutters problems_found with five self-retracted or duplicate non-findings plus a spec

Output_B lands two genuine catches (imprecise 'removal', src-012/013 date issue) but leaves the date issue u

Both correctly catch the one hard factual error (src-002 names Knezevic, not Andrija Mandic) and both flag the src-004 November-2025 date divergence and the unconfirmed death toll. Output A over-corrects: it degrades the correct 'November 2024' to vague 'late 2024' and deletes the true, uncontradicted 'killing fifteen people,' damaging accurate content, whereas B correctly reasons that Nov 2024 is

Output B's three findings are all real and precise: the misattributed FLNKS-president name, the unsupported 'democratic correction' phrasing, and the Bougival 'now-rejected' claim wrongly cited to src-017 instead of src-001. Output A catches the name issue but its temporal 'trigger' finding (problems 1 and 4, one a self-admitted duplicate) mischaracterizes the article, which attributes the 2024 un

Output A pads problems_found with three self-retracted non-issues and mischaracterizes two excerpts (the article's 'follows' already sequences the PHEIC before June 29, and src-009's summary attributes the framing to AFP, not AHF as A claims — the AHF inference rests only on the URL slug), though its AFP/AHF catch has some merit. Output B is narrow but every assertion is accurate and well-supporte

Both are fabrication-free and correctly identify and fix the real 25-vs-29 casualty divergence flagged in preliminary_divergences. A adds a valid catch (the omitted 'up to 400' Taliban hospital-toll claim in src-020) but pads its list with two items mislabeled 'factually_incorrect' that it then concedes need no correction. Output B identifies a real, substantive gap — the article omits the robotics-initiative pillar (src-004) and the 1,450 trillion won headline figure (src-013) — and fixes it accurately with correct math and source support. Both correctly flag the Kubayi/Lamola dual 'Justice Minister' problem with sound corrections and neither fabricates (B's cabinet-reshuffle note is explicitly hedged, its one weak item is self-retracted). Both are well-grounded with no fabrications: A correctly catches the 34-year-old/lived-at-facility citation mismatch and the unsupported second-person-in-custody claim, while B uniquely catches two subtler real issues A missed (the Spanish-language pseudo-verbatim quote from a German-speaking police chief via El Pais, and the article over-attributing the list of prior shootings to DW/src-003). Neither output fabricates: A's two applied fixes (Abu Zerega 'predominantly Zaghawa' vs sources' 'non-Arab ethnic groups', and Wallenberg 'led by' vs 'coalition of') are warranted, though its problems_found is padded with six self-negating 'no error' entries, and its Yagoub explanation is muddled. B's single catch — the unsupported '200-page' count, absent from every source — is a real, sharp find
